title: USVI initiative working meeting with Mark Newson, Malcolm Wilson, and Telly Renfroe
summary: Meeting confirms severe USVI recovery execution bottlenecks despite large allocated capital. Participants reported approximately $24B initial funding context, with prior award flow issues and funds returned (~$900M) due to delivery and scope constraints. A major blocker is workforce accommodation and boots-on-ground capacity, with claims of 8,000 units backlog and ~11,000 accommodations needed. Mark Newson positioned as a high-access operator with direct government ties, scope optimization capability, and potential to facilitate low-cost land access for workforce camp deployment across three islands.
decisions: Prioritize workforce housing/man-camp architecture as a central unlock for disaster-recovery capital deployment.
Treat local financing and accommodation structure as a near-term entry wedge rather than broad developer execution framing.
Maintain active engagement channel with Mark through early April visit window.
risks: Capital attrition risk: reported remainder could be lost if awards and delivery do not accelerate.
Execution risk: large contractors constrained and costs passed downstream to local subcontractors.
Labor/housing bottleneck risk: insufficient accommodation and manpower slows all major pacts.
Governance/scope risk: repeated rescoping and delayed infrastructure delivery across critical assets.

next_actions.action: Validate reported funding numbers and returned-funds timeline (including ~$900M reference) with primary program documents.
Draft USVI workforce accommodation/man-camp deployment concept with transition path to permanent affordable housing.
Prepare land-use pathway brief based on claim of government-available acreage and potential $1 lease structure.
Set pre-brief packet for Mark before first week of April travel window.

title: Refocus Afrika meeting
summary: Refocus Afrika presented itself as a long-running, high-access Africa operator with senior government relationships and private-deployment orientation. Discussion emphasized active pipelines in infrastructure, energy, transport, EV, and manufacturing across multiple countries, with Tanzania and southern Africa highlighted as immediate access lanes. Team indicated willingness to onboard USG and broker direct introductions to project owners and government stakeholders.
decisions: Proceed with structured onboarding flow with Refocus Afrika for government/stakeholder introductions.
Prioritize Tanzania as an initial coordination lane due to claimed institutional access and active project flow.
Treat public-profile low visibility as a diligence variable; rely on document/evidence checks over announcements.
risks: Verification risk: many claims are relationship-based and intentionally low-publicity.
Concentration risk: access model appears reliant on key individuals and political relationships.
Execution risk: multi-country pipeline breadth may outpace verifiable deployment evidence.
FX/convertibility risk remains material despite stated improvements in certain markets.

next_actions.action: Run structured diligence packet: verify flagship project references (Zimbabwe solar, Cyber City, Ethiopia EV/charging, rail and stadium claims).
Prepare onboarding brief and intro checklist for Refocus Afrika-led government/stakeholder meetings.
Build Tanzania-first engagement map: ministries, project owners, and decision interfaces.
Define evidence threshold for private/no-press counterparties before mandate advancement.

source.notes: User-provided meeting minutes + leadership page link

title: Wade Cherwayko meeting
summary: Discussion centered on Angola as a leading location for data center and sovereign AI infrastructure due to significant hydropower surplus, existing fiber/cable partnerships, and claimed readiness across land, power, and EPC execution. Wade indicated in-country operating access with government interfaces, while identifying capital stack closure and top-tier technology partnership as primary bottlenecks.
decisions: Treat Angola as a priority validation lane for AI/data-center infrastructure feasibility.
Frame immediate diligence around prepayment-linked commitment structure (power, fiber, land).
Position Wade as potential on-ground coordination lead if counterparties are qualified.
risks: Capital stack remains unresolved and may block conversion despite technical readiness claims.
Technology partner dependency is critical; no final anchor partner confirmed.
Commitment terms reportedly hinge on upfront prepayments, increasing execution risk if sequencing fails.
Reliance on MoUs/exclusivity terms requires legal and enforceability verification.

next_actions.action: Run structured diligence on Angola package: power agreement, land rights, fiber terms, EPC credentials, and exclusivity/MoU enforceability.
Map capital stack options and funding sequence against stated prepayment requirements ($87M power, ~$18M fiber, land commitment).
Define target profile and outreach list for anchor technology partners to close partner-side bottleneck.
Clarify Wade's operating role, scope, and governance boundaries for government/partner coordination.

title: Angola Cables meeting
summary: Meeting positioned Angola Cables as a sovereign-controlled digital backbone asset with intercontinental routes (Africa-Brazil-Europe-US), hyperscaler/OTT relationships, and strategic leverage for data center and AI infrastructure deployment. Core operating takeaway: infrastructure availability exists, but participation and value capture are increasingly gated by sovereign approval, governance control, and scarce fiber-pair allocation timelines.
decisions: Treat Angola Cables as a potential control-point partner rather than only a vendor layer.
Prioritize governance-access strategy in parallel with commercial/fiber negotiations.
Advance diligence on near-term fiber reservation structure due to reported market scarcity.
risks: Regulatory/sovereign gate risk: participation rights are state-mediated.
Commercial scarcity risk: active competition for fiber pairs may compress decision window.
Counterparty concentration risk around key relationship channels.
Short-term contract bridge risk if long-term structure is not locked in time.

next_actions.action: Validate route/control claims and commercial terms with primary Angola Cables documents (capacity, rights, pricing, term flexibility).
Prepare governance-access map for Angola telecom approval layer and decision rights for new entrants.
Assess proposed ~US$15M down-payment reservation structure versus alternative entry mechanisms and risk controls.
Define shortlist of anchor tenant/partner profiles required to convert short-term fiber access into long-term strategic position.

title: USG Finance Meeting
summary: Liquidity and fiscal stress review covering Bestaf debt exposure (~$1.5M), April payment pause communications, payroll/benefit constraints, insurance burden, tax/underwriting blockers for LOC, and contingency planning.
decisions: Issue written April non-payment notices before month-end.
Pursue extension on Bestaf dev-team payments.
Advance contingency plan for UDG fiscal responsibility and financing readiness.
risks: Near-term cash runway compression (end-April projected ~$20k).
Debt and vendor payment default risk.
Compliance risk around payroll/benefits and tax arrears.
Underwriter rejection risk without income/returns package.

next_actions.action: Get extension on Bestaf dev-team payments.
Collect vendor list from Sico.
Collect agreement package from Monica.
Have Leo conversation and put outcomes in writing.

title: Mark G Meeting
summary: Relationship-intelligence meeting focused on USVI legal/governance and federal-adjacent connector pathways, including Joel H. Holt and additional network nodes pending verification.
decisions: Map Mark-network legal/governance connectors into actor model as identified (verification-pending).
Prioritize identity verification for placeholder contacts and federal-role claims.
risks: Attribution risk if names/roles are not independently verified.
Over-reliance risk on relationship-only access channels.

next_actions.action: Verify identities/roles for Mark-network actors added from meeting notes.
Prioritize USVI legal/governance connectors for pathway mapping.

title: Meeting with Tanisha Lewis
summary: Network and pathway meeting highlighting Tanisha’s bipartisan government access, USVI familiarity, organizational-change experience, and advisory/connector network including Don Bacon, Steven Benjamin, Tonya Saunders, Hannibal Ware, and Mrs Kim (identity pending).
decisions: Map Tanisha network as first-degree/second-degree actors under Tanisha ownership.
Prioritize near-term VI connector handoff via Mrs Kim and energy-policy linkage review.
risks: Identity/title verification gaps for newly introduced contacts.
Execution timing risk if follow-up introductions slip beyond one-week window.

next_actions.action: Confirm full identity and role for Mrs Kim.
Verify Hannibal Ware availability and pathway relevance after return.
Compile first outreach sequence for Tanisha network nodes.

title: Invest Africa roundtable alignment meeting
summary: Meeting aligned around an Africa roundtable focused on two primary constraints: capital and connectivity. The room is intended to be curated and high-signal, with ecosystem participation expected across U.S. government, DFIs, telecom/fintech operators, institutional investors, banks/corporates, policymakers, Amazon, and infrastructure leaders. Flutterwave is a lead anchor partner with GB identified as participant, while Dr. Bosun Tijani remains unconfirmed. Commercial pathways discussed include Invest Africa membership ($10K/year), principal roundtable sponsorship (~$25K), and potential co-curated webinars. Confidence in event execution is high (~95%), with Shannon identified as the decision authority on partner format.
decisions: Center roundtable framing on Africa's capital-and-connectivity bottlenecks.
Proceed with participation posture as roundtable partner pending Shannon's final yes criteria.
Treat Flutterwave as lead anchor; GB is named participant.
Keep room deliberately curated and smaller-format.
Coordinate internally with Dare and Sayyu on discussion scope with fallback tracks defined.
risks: Final participation criteria from Shannon not yet confirmed.
Outcome definition risk if execution outputs are not codified in advance.
Post-event momentum risk without tracked follow-through and ownership.
Participant confirmation risk for currently unconfirmed strategic attendees.

next_actions.action: Confirm what a final yes from Shannon requires this week.
Align on 5 priority attendees for direct post-session engagement.
Confirm whether USG can shape a segment around execution pathways rather than broad narratives.
Confirm support for a capital-to-execution working block with concrete outputs.
Co-define 2-3 required outcomes for the room.
Confirm whether curated 1:1s can be pre-scheduled within 7 days post-event.
Confirm Invest Africa's follow-on tracking model to prevent momentum loss.
Confirm whether Invest Africa will support a joint follow-up memo with owners, deadlines, and next calls.
Finalize USG deliverables, deadlines, and bilateral success criteria.

source.notes: User-provided meeting minutes in Telegram; answered items retained as notes/decisions and unanswered items mapped to next actions.

title: Angola AI Campus meeting
summary: Team confirmed core infrastructure readiness signals for the Angola AI campus lane, including executed power agreement, completed power MOU pathway (with contract conversion pending), confirmed construction partner, and existing fiber footprint. Strategic posture is now time-compressed: Angolan presidential support is being pursued as an executive-level decision, with expectation that capital stack and technical partner alignment be secured within 30 days (by end of April). Commercial architecture discussed a prepayment power-capacity lock model (initial $10M non-refundable prepayment credit), plus urgent fiber monetization/ownership timing tied to a 60-day decision window. USG was positioned as a relationship and orchestration layer across hyperscalers/cloud AI counterparties and family-office capital across the US, Africa, and the Middle East. Meeting tone emphasized execution discipline and no-misstep delivery ahead of a potential cabinet-level presidential presentation.
decisions: Treat end-of-April as hard deadline for capital stack and technical-partner readiness.
Advance contract conversion from MOU-level to executable commercial agreements.
Prioritize immediate fiber monetization path and ownership capture under compressed timeline.
Position USG in a JV/support role contingent on technical and financial backing letters from hyperscaler-adjacent counterparties.
risks: Timeline risk: failure to secure stack and partners inside 30 days may collapse presidential-level momentum.
Commercial prepayment risk: initial $10M power lock payment is non-refundable.
Fiber capture risk: 60-day monetization window may be lost to competing buyers.
Execution risk: high-visibility government exposure increases consequence of sequencing errors.

next_actions.action: Secure financing required to monetize and secure fiber line rights under stated 60-day window.
Receive updated payment outlay and sequencing package from Wade.
Convert power MOU pathway into binding contract package and validate prepayment terms.
Assemble technical/financial support-letter package and trip cohort for potential Angola presidential meeting.

title: Eugene Yun meeting
summary: Meeting focused on building a Korea-to-Nigeria industrial collaboration path, anchored on identifying a low-friction first project and clarifying Dangote priorities. Eugene outlined a high-access convening model to host senior Nigerian principals in Korea, with structured introductions to captains of Korean industry and finance across Samsung, SK Group, LG, Lotte Group, and GS Group. Team discussed using a September conference window and designing a short, high-density meeting sprint (up to 15 strategic meetings over 4-7 days), with media and potential presidential-office participation to increase profile and conversion. Additional technical collaboration lanes included refinery audit/review support, smart ID systems, robotics/drone inspection, and big-data solutions. Eugene also committed to review the UDG Banking deck.
decisions: Prioritize identifying the easiest executable project in Nigeria with Korean companies.
Develop a targeted Korea visit concept for Dangote-side principals tied to a September conference window.
Use a concentrated executive-introduction format (4-7 days, high-density meetings) to accelerate partnership formation.
Position GS Group and other Korean groups as technical/operational solution partners for refinery and digital modernization lanes.
risks: Priority ambiguity risk if Dangote strategic intent is not clearly defined early.
Conversion risk if high-level introductions are not translated into scoped project workstreams.
Scheduling risk around September conference timing and principal availability.
Execution risk from broad solution scope without a narrowly defined first pilot.

next_actions.action: Identify the easiest executable Nigeria project to launch with Korean counterparties.
Clarify and document Dangote priority asks for Korea engagement.
Draft September Korea visit plan including 4-7 day agenda and ~15 strategic meetings.
Define GS Group-led refinery review/audit scope and candidate smart systems workstream.
Review UDG Banking deck and return comments.

title: USG + Bestaf alignment meeting
summary: Bestaf paused active activity pending a reset on economics and transparency. Both sides reaffirmed partnership intent, but counterparties require auditable substantiation of USG's claimed value contribution and a shared valuation logic to map contribution against the contracted 15% ownership position. Core disagreement is not relationship intent; it is conversion logic (credits/partnership/development value vs cash-equivalent contribution) plus governance visibility.
decisions: Treat current blocker as an economic-structure and governance-transparency issue, not strategic-fit failure.
Provide a formal evidence package of investments, invoices, transfers, licenses/technology contribution, and partner-linked value claims.
Address valuation baseline explicitly before further equity-percentage discussions.
Proceed under existing signed contract framework while clarifying substitution/capital expectations.
risks: Trust and credibility erosion if contribution evidence is not delivered quickly and clearly.
Governance risk from key stakeholder visibility gaps (Chairman/legal not fully aligned on prior decisions).
Economic-structure deadlock risk without agreed valuation baseline and value-to-equity conversion method.
Execution stall risk due to suspended activity while legal/leadership reassess.

next_actions.action: Deliver auditable value substantiation pack (invoices, transfers, software spend evidence, and partner credit documentation) to Bestaf leadership/legal.
Request and document Bestaf valuation basis (current and/or 2024 baseline) to anchor 15% economics discussion.
Produce side-by-side framework: cash investment, credits, discounts, licenses, and development value with explicit equity-treatment assumptions.
Run legal/commercial alignment session to reconcile contract substitution terms and capital-contribution expectations.

source.notes: User-provided meeting minutes PDF in Telegram

title: Meeting with Angella Goren
summary: Strategy meeting on positioning USG as a GDP-growth and community infrastructure enabler, with potential collaboration lanes across fintech, loyalty rails, government/trade access, and brand-backed local economic activation. Angella shared RNTR/TapNGo progress, target launch timing (Q3 with September rental-unit rollout), and expansion intent across Canada, MENA, Africa, and UK/US corridors.
decisions: Evaluate RNTR/TapNGo as a potential platform partnership lane for community credit-building and local commerce activation.
Review Angella's RNTR pitch deck upon receipt and assess fit with USG mandate architecture.
Open partner exploration around March Networks, Microsoft, and global brand channels for measurable community-impact deployment.
Treat government/trade-organization relationships (commissioners/ambassadors) as a parallel access lane for international rollout.
risks: Operational scaling risk across cities/countries (team quality, consistency, and attrition control).
Regulatory and licensing timeline risk as expansion accelerates.
Execution risk if KPI measurement framework for brand/community outcomes is not standardized early.
Partnership-conversion risk if strategic intros are not translated into contracted deployment pathways.

next_actions.action: Send RNTR pitch deck and latest website package for diligence.
Run fit review of RNTR/TapNGo against USG capital-deployment architecture and identify entry point.
Draft partnership outreach short-list (March Networks, Microsoft, selected global brands) with KPI-first framing.
Define government/trade-relations engagement track for Africa and other target markets with named connectors.

title: Barclay Knapp meeting
summary: Working session on telecom/satellite capital-markets strategy for Nigeria corridor execution, focused on reverse-takeover (RTO) pathways via London or Toronto as potentially faster listing routes than current U.S. market conditions. Discussion emphasized pairing a suitable operating mobile/infrastructure target with a credible shell sponsor, conservative regulator-first packaging, and early capitalization to clear listing and 18-month runway requirements.
decisions: Prioritize LSE/TSE RTO track as primary listing strategy, with NYSE treated as lower-probability near term due to current political/regulatory friction.
Run parallel shell-screening process (London and Toronto) to avoid single-counterparty dependency.
Start target-company search for a qualified Nigerian mobile-adjacent operating nucleus with clean financials and revenue scale.
Structure pitch around commercial economics, payments architecture, and conservative regulator-first disclosures rather than subsidy framing.
risks: Execution risk if acquisition target lacks clean audited IFRS financials or operational maturity.
Counterparty quality/politics risk in shell and sponsor selection.
Market-timing risk from macro/geopolitical shocks disrupting RTO window.
Free-float/control-group compliance risk if ownership structure is not engineered to exchange thresholds.
Funding risk if minimum working-capital demonstration and 18-month runway proof are not in place.

next_actions.action: Build shortlist of Nigerian mobile/mobile-adjacent acquisition targets ($10M-$20M revenue, clean 2+ year financials, scalable ops).
Launch dual beauty-contest process for qualified LSE/TSE shell and sponsor candidates.
Draft regulator-first transaction architecture (LOI/MOU format, trading-halt sequence, prospectus prep, working-capital proof).
Validate Fieldbase fit as merge/acquisition candidate and map governance/ownership implications.
